seo.title: Building Code Generation with Google ADK + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build code generation with Google ADK + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building code generation agents with Google's Agent Development Kit (ADK) enables powerful LLM-driven development workflows using Gemini models. However, multi-agent delegation, sandbox execution, and iterative refinement introduce critical risks like circular dependencies, credential leakage, and unsafe code execution. Vex provides essential guardrails to ensure reliability, safety, and consistency in these high-risk scenarios.

architectureOverview.heading: Code Generation Architecture with Google ADK
architectureOverview.description:
The typical Google ADK code generation architecture features a lead agent powered by Gemini that receives requirements and delegates to specialized sub-agents: a code generator, executor, tester, and reviewer. The code generator produces Python/JavaScript code, which the executor runs in a sandboxed environment. Test validation agents check functionality while a reviewer ensures safety and best practices. Iterative refinement loops continue until requirements are met.

Session state management across agents is critical, with shared memory storing code versions, test results, and execution logs. Gemini model switching between agents can cause output inconsistencies, while tool response mismatches between executor and tester agents create integration failures. Vex monitoring at the before_agent_callback ensures each agent's inputs/outputs remain consistent and safe.

The workflow handles dependency management, credential isolation, and syntax validation through structured tool calls, making it powerful but requiring robust reliability layers.

risks.name: Multi-agent circular dependencies
risks.description: Code generator delegates to executor, which calls back to generator creating infinite loops
risks.mitigation: Vex dependency graph analysis in before_agent_callback blocks circular delegation

risks.name: Unsafe code execution
risks.description: Generated code contains syscalls, file writes, or credential access bypassing sandbox
risks.mitigation: Vex code safety scanner rejects dangerous patterns before sandbox execution

risks.name: Credential leakage in generated code
risks.description: Agents embed API keys or secrets in code during iterative refinement
risks.mitigation: Vex credential detector strips secrets from code and agent memory

risks.name: Gemini output inconsistency
risks.description: Model switching between agents produces different code styles and formats
risks.mitigation: Vex output normalization enforces consistent code structure across agents

implementation.title: Basic ADK Code Generation Agent
implementation.description: Core agent structure with code generation, execution, and validation workflow

implementation.title: Multi-Agent Delegation Setup
implementation.description: Lead agent coordinates generator, executor, tester, and reviewer sub-agents
implementation.code:
from google_adk.orchestrator import Orchestrator

orchestrator = Orchestrator([
    CodeGenerator(),
    CodeExecutor(),
    TestValidator(),
    CodeReviewer()
])

async def code_generation_pipeline(requirements: str):
    workflow = orchestrator.create_workflow([
        ('generate', 'CodeGenerator'),
        ('execute', 'CodeExecutor'),
        ('validate', 'TestValidator'),
        ('review', 'CodeReviewer'),
    ])
    result = await workflow.run(requirements)
    return result

implementation.title: Sandbox Execution Tool
implementation.description: Secure execution environment with dependency and safety checks

implementation.title: Iterative Refinement Loop
implementation.description: Continues refinement until tests pass and safety checks complete
implementation.code:
async def iterative_code_gen(requirements: str, max_iterations: int = 5):
    state = {'code': '', 'tests_passed': False, 'iteration': 0}
    
    while not state['tests_passed'] and state['iteration'] < max_iterations:
        state['iteration'] += 1
        
        # Generate
        code = await code_generator.generate(requirements, state)
        
        # Execute & Test
        exec_result = await sandbox.execute(code)
        test_result = await validator.test(code, exec_result)
        
        if test_result['passes']:
            state['tests_passed'] = True
            state['code'] = code
        else:
            requirements += f" Fix: {test_result['errors']}"
    
    return state

vexIntegration.title: Vex before_agent_callback Setup
vexIntegration.description: Install Vex and configure as callback to monitor all agent interactions
vexIntegration.code:
import vex

from google_adk.orchestrator import Orchestrator

# Initialize Vex client
vex_client = vex.Client(api_key="your-vex-api-key")

@vex_client.guardrail(
    risk_profile="high",
    verifications=[
        "code_safety",
        "syntax_validity",
        "no_credential_leakage",
        "execution_sandboxing",
        "dependency_safety"
    ]
)
def before_agent_callback(agent_id: str, input_data: dict) -> dict:
    """Vex guardrail for all ADK agents"""
    
    # Check for circular dependencies
    if vex_client.detect_circular_dependency(agent_id, input_data):
        raise Exception("Circular dependency detected")
    
    # Code safety scan
    if 'code' in input_data:
        vex_client.scan_code_safety(input_data['code'])
    
    return input_data

# Apply to orchestrator
orchestrator = Orchestrator(agents, before_agent_callback=before_agent_callback)
